title: Visual Signaling (Daytime)
immediate_steps: Build three fires in a triangle formation (the universal distress signal)
Once a plane or helicopter is heard, throw green leaves, pine boughs, or damp wood on the fire to create thick white smoke
Use a signal mirror or shiny object (CD, foil) to flash sunlight at passing planes or distant ridges
Create a large 'X' or 'SOS' on the ground using highly contrasting materials (dark rocks on snow, or bright clothes on dirt)
avoid: Do not light signal fires until you hear or see potential rescuers
Do not point a laser pointer at aircraft (it is illegal and blinding)

title: Lost In Forest - Signaling And Rescue
immediate_steps: Increase visibility for rescuers once a safe location is established
Use whistles, mirrors, flashlights or bright-colored clothing for signaling
Create visible ground markers using stones, branches or logs in open areas
Smoke from controlled fires may assist rescuers during daytime
Stay near signaling locations and respond immediately to sounds of rescue teams or vehicles
avoid: Avoid unnecessary movement once a safe location is established
